deep_tilling.pre: Deep tilling loosens soil for easy planting but disrupts soil structure.
deep_tilling.post: Tilling breaks up soil aggregates, leading to erosion and destroying the microbiome.
deep_tilling.realWorldAnchor: Intensive tilling can reduce soil organic carbon by up to 30% over 10 years.

no_till.pre: Leaving soil undisturbed preserves organic matter and soil structure.
no_till.post: Soil health increased. You preserved the fungal networks that help roots absorb nutrients.
no_till.realWorldAnchor: No-till farming can increase soil carbon sequestration by 0.3 tons per acre annually.

soil_test.pre: Testing soil reveals exact nutrient deficiencies before planting.
soil_test.post: You learned your exact soil needs. This unlocks precision application in the fertilizer stage.
soil_test.realWorldAnchor: Soil testing prevents over-application, reducing fertilizer costs by 15-20% on average.

monoculture.pre: Growing one crop maximizes harvest efficiency but creates vulnerability.
monoculture.post: Biodiversity dropped. You created a perfect feast for a single type of pest.
monoculture.realWorldAnchor: Monocultures often require 2-3x more pesticide use than polyculture systems.

polyculture.pre: Growing multiple crops together creates natural synergies.
polyculture.post: Biodiversity increased. Natural predators are now managing your pest population.
polyculture.realWorldAnchor: Intercropping can increase overall yield stability by up to 20% compared to monocultures.

crop_rotation.pre: Alternating crops breaks pest cycles and balances nutrient uptake.
crop_rotation.post: Soil health improved. Different root depths are breaking up compacted soil layers.
crop_rotation.realWorldAnchor: Rotating crops can increase subsequent corn yields by 10-15% compared to continuous planting.

synthetic_npk.pre: Synthetic fertilizers deliver nutrients fast but break down soil microbiomes over time.
synthetic_npk.post: High yield locked in, but soil biology took a hit. Watch out for compaction.
synthetic_npk.realWorldAnchor: Real farms using synthetic NPK produce ~30% more yield but see 40% more soil erosion over 10 years.

organic_compost.pre: Compost releases nutrients slowly and feeds the soil food web.
organic_compost.post: Microbiome is thriving. Yields won't peak immediately, but the soil will hold water better.
organic_compost.realWorldAnchor: Adding organic compost can increase soil water-holding capacity by up to 10,000 gallons per acre.

precision_app.pre: Applying exact nutrients based on soil mapping.
precision_app.post: You gave the plants exactly what they needed and nothing more.
precision_app.realWorldAnchor: Precision agriculture technologies can reduce fertilizer runoff by up to 50%.

flood_irrigation.pre: Flooding the field is simple but wastes massive amounts of water.
flood_irrigation.post: Water table dropped visibly. Much of the water evaporated before reaching roots.
flood_irrigation.realWorldAnchor: Flood irrigation is only about 40-50% efficient, losing the rest to evaporation and runoff.

drip_irrigation.pre: Delivers water directly to the root zone, maximizing efficiency.
drip_irrigation.post: Water table preserved. If your soil is healthy, plants will thrive.
drip_irrigation.realWorldAnchor: Drip irrigation systems can achieve 90% water use efficiency.

machine_harvest.post: Harvest complete. Heavy machinery caused some soil compaction.
machine_harvest.realWorldAnchor: Heavy tractor traffic can compact soil up to 2 feet deep, reducing future root growth.

crop_waste_composting.pre: Incorporating harvest residue back into the soil system.
crop_waste_composting.post: You returned significant organic matter to the field. Sustainability maximized.
crop_waste_composting.realWorldAnchor: Leaving crop residue on the field decreases wind and water erosion by up to 60%.

stageTransitions.1: The land is prepared. The choices you made for the soil foundation will cascade into the growing season.
stageTransitions.2: Seeds are in the ground. Pests are watching your layout.
stageTransitions.3: Plants are feeding. Nutrients are moving through the soil profile...
stageTransitions.4: Water is flowing. The aquifer level changes based on your delivery method.
stageTransitions.5: The season is over. The farm report awaits.
